record.field: Discourse is a modern, open-source community discussion platform that powers forums, mailing lists and real-time chat from a single self-hosted application.

Discourse is a modern, open-source community discussion platform that powers forums, mailing lists and real-time chat from a single self-hosted application.

It lets organizations and communities host threaded long-form discussions, with topics, categories, trust levels and moderation tools built in. Beyond classic forums it works as a mailing list (post and reply by email) and includes built-in real-time chat, making it a flexible home for support communities, product feedback and internal collaboration. Its extensive theme and plugin ecosystem, including AI-assisted features, lets teams tailor it to their needs while keeping full control of their data.

Key features
  • Threaded discussion forum with categories, tags and configurable trust levels
  • Built-in real-time chat alongside long-form topics
  • Full email integration: reply by email and run it as a mailing list
  • Powerful moderation, anti-spam and reputation tooling
  • Rich plugin and theme ecosystem, including AI-assisted moderation and content tools
  • Security by default: CSP enabled out of the box and strong password hashing (PBKDF2-SHA256)
Best for

Teams and organizations that need a self-hosted community hub: open-source projects, SaaS product feedback and support forums, customer and developer communities, and internal discussion spaces where owning the data and infrastructure matters.

services: discourse-db: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/postgresql:17 volumes: - ./discourse_postgresql_data:/bitnami/postgresql environment: POSTGRESQL_USERNAME: bn_discourse POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} POSTGRESQL_DATABASE: bitnami_discourse healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U bn_discourse -d bitnami_discourse interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_discourse_db' networks: - internal discourse-redis: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/redis:7.4 volumes: - ./discourse_redis_data:/bitnami/redis environment: REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD} healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - -a - ${REDIS_PASSWORD} - ping interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_discourse_redis' networks: - internal discourse-app: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/discourse:3.5.0 volumes: - ./discourse_data:/bitnami/discourse depends_on: discourse-db: condition: service_healthy discourse-redis: condition: service_healthy environment: DISCOURSE_HOST: '{{ DOMAIN }}' DISCOURSE_DATABASE_HOST: discourse-db DISCOURSE_DATABASE_PORT_NUMBER: 5432 DISCOURSE_DATABASE_USER: bn_discourse DISCOURSE_DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} DISCOURSE_DATABASE_NAME: bitnami_discourse DISCOURSE_REDIS_HOST: discourse-redis DISCOURSE_REDIS_PORT_NUMBER: 6379 DISCOURSE_REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD} restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network discourse-sidekiq: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/discourse:3.5.0 volumes: - ./discourse_sidekiq_data:/bitnami/discourse depends_on: - discourse-app command: /opt/bitnami/scripts/discourse-sidekiq/run.sh environment: DISCOURSE_HOST: '{{ DOMAIN }}' DISCOURSE_DATABASE_HOST: discourse-db DISCOURSE_DATABASE_PORT_NUMBER: 5432 DISCOURSE_DATABASE_USER: bn_discourse DISCOURSE_DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} DISCOURSE_DATABASE_NAME: bitnami_discourse DISCOURSE_REDIS_HOST: discourse-redis DISCOURSE_REDIS_PORT_NUMBER: 6379 DISCOURSE_REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD} restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_discourse_sidekiq' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T discourse-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d discourse-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T discourse-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T discourse-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T discourse-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
